findings.item: Use bounded queues and load-aware admission at audio input
findings.issue: Line 226 is `self._audio_in_queue = asyncio.Queue()` — an UNBOUNDED asyncio.Queue (no maxsize). grep of the file confirms no maxsize is ever set. The item advocates 'bounded queues' but the canonical clone citation shows an explicitly unbounded queue, so it demonstrates the queue location, not the bounded-queue/backpressure mechanism. The load-aware admission half (worker.py:778) is correct. The item hedges 'queue bounds are deployment-specific', so this is weak rather than flatly wrong.
findings.correct: Note that base_input.py:226 is an unbounded asyncio.Queue(); no OSS clone bounds the audio-in queue. The load-aware admission citation (livekit worker.py:778) is the only canonical backpressure primitive.

summary: Section 7 Reliability is overwhelmingly accurate. All numeric targets verified against cited code: REL-01 exponential_backoff_time min_wait=4/max_wait=10 (network.py:11) and max_retries=3 (websocket_service.py:85); LiveKit retry min(n*2,10) (worker.py:1096); REL-02 _MIN_STABLE_CONNECTION_DURATION=5.0 and _MAX_CONSECUTIVE_QUICK_FAILURES=3 (websocket_service.py:35-36, detection at 166-189); REL-05 HEARTBEAT_SECS=1.0/HEARTBEAT_MONITOR_SECS=10.0 (worker.py:87-88) and AUDIO_INPUT_TIMEOUT_SECS=0.5 (base_input.py:31); REL-07 drain_timeout=1800 (worker.py:203) and drain() at 872-900; REL-08 stt fallback availability/_try_recovery/stt_availability_changed (95-103, 175-248). REL-04 send_with_retry replay (122-138) and STT linear-timestamp offset (stt.py:390-398) confirmed. REL-06 push_error fatal flag + TTS FallbackAdapter confirmed. REL-10 Deepgram source has a 'Common Failure Modes' section and metrics/base.py:20-105 carries per-layer telemetry. All Evidence note IDs (REL-01,02,04,05,06,07,08,09,10, STT-07, OBS-06) exist in notes/. Only one MINOR: the 'bounded queues' canonical citation points at an unbounded asyncio.Queue().
